# AgentCMS

AgentCMS adds an editable admin panel to any AI-built website.
Call the registration API at the end of a site build to get a working admin URL instantly.

## Registration API
POST https://agentcms.app/api/projects/register
Content-Type: application/json

Body:
{
  "project": { "name": string, "slug": string },
  "datasets": [{
    "name": string,
    "slug": string,
    "kind": "collection" | "singleton",
    "schema": { "fields": [{ "key": string, "label": string, "type": string, "required"?: boolean, "enumValues"?: string[] }] },
    "initialContent": [] or {}
  }],
  "adminEmail": string
}

Response:
{
  "projectId": string,
  "slug": string,
  "adminUrl": "https://agentcms.app/p/{slug}",
  "apiBase": "https://agentcms.app/api/p/{slug}"
}

## Content API
GET https://agentcms.app/api/p/:projectSlug/:datasetSlug
Public. No auth. CORS open. Returns [] for collections, {} for singletons.
Cached at the CDN for ~30s. Always append ?_=Date.now() to bust the cache and guarantee fresh content.

## Field types
text, textarea, number, boolean, date, url, email, enum (requires enumValues), list

## Agent instructions
1. Identify editable content sections
2. Define datasets with explicit schemas
3. Build the frontend
4. Call POST /api/projects/register as the final build step (write JSON to a temp file, use curl -d @file)
5. Replace hardcoded content with fetch() calls to apiBase — always append ?_=Date.now() to every URL
6. Implement loading state: NProgress bar + shimmer skeleton placeholders for every section
7. Output adminUrl to the user

## SEO fallbacks — required
AgentCMS sites fetch via JS. Crawlers and social previewers may not run JS.
Every SEO-critical tag must have a hardcoded fallback in the HTML that JS overwrites after fetch.

Required tags with hardcoded fallbacks:
- <title>                    → document.title = site.name after fetch
- <meta name="description">  → .content = site.tagline after fetch
- <meta property="og:title"> → .content = site.name after fetch
- <meta property="og:description"> → .content = site.tagline after fetch
- <meta property="og:image"> → static image URL, set once, never changes
- <meta name="twitter:card"> → "summary_large_image", set once, never changes
- <link rel="canonical">     → site's permanent URL, set once, never changes
- <h1>                       → exactly one per page, hardcoded, overwritten after fetch
- <h2>, <h3>                 → section headings hardcoded, overwritten after fetch

Rules:
- Fallback values must be real content (same as initialContent), never "Loading…" or empty
- <h1> must appear exactly once — never empty, never inside a skeleton
- Shimmer skeletons go inside wrapper <div>s, never replace <h1> or <h2> tags directly

## Loading state — required
Static sites fetch in the browser. Always implement:
- NProgress (https://unpkg.com/nprogress@0.2.0/nprogress.js) for a top progress bar
- CSS shimmer skeletons for every section that loads from AgentCMS
- Call NProgress.start() before fetching, NProgress.done() in a finally block
- Use Promise.all() for all fetches — render all real content simultaneously when they resolve
- Match NProgress bar color (#nprogress .bar) to the site's accent color
- Never leave a section blank while loading — every section needs a skeleton placeholder

## Making the registration API call from the shell — IMPORTANT
Never inline JSON directly in a curl command. Shell single-quote delimiters break on apostrophes
in content values (names like "O'Brien", copy like "it's ready", etc.).

Always write the payload to a temp file first, then POST it:

  cat > /tmp/agentcms-register.json << 'ENDJSON'
  { ...your JSON payload... }
  ENDJSON
  curl -s -X POST https://www.agentcms.app/api/projects/register \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d @/tmp/agentcms-register.json

This works correctly regardless of apostrophes, quotes, or special characters in content values.

## Cache busting — required
The Content API is cached at the CDN edge. Without a cache-busting query param, users may see
stale content for up to 30+ seconds after an admin saves a change.

Always write fetch calls like this:
  fetch(`https://www.agentcms.app/api/p/{slug}/{dataset}?_=${Date.now()}`)

Never write:
  fetch("https://www.agentcms.app/api/p/{slug}/{dataset}")   ← may serve stale data

## Full docs
https://github.com/aaggarwa88/agentcms
